content: Landmark statin trials established cardiovascular benefit: 4S (simvastatin post-MI—30% mortality reduction), WOSCOPS (pravastatin primary prevention), LIPID (pravastatin ACS), HPS (simvastatin high-risk patients—benefit regardless of baseline LDL), TNT (atorvastatin 80mg vs 10mg post-ACS—intensive better), IMPROVE-IT (ezetimibe added to statin—modest additional benefit), FOURIER (evolocumab PCSK9 inhibitor added to statin—further LDL reduction and CV benefit), REDUCE-IT (icosapent ethyl added to statin—reduced CV events). Meta-analyses show each 1 mmol/L (39 mg/dL) LDL reduction yields ~22% relative risk reduction in major vascular events. Statins recommended for: clinical ASCVD (secondary prevention), LDL ≥190 mg/dL (familial hypercholesterolemia), diabetes age 40-75 with LDL 70-189, primary prevention with 10-year ASCVD risk ≥7.5% (consider if 5-7.5%). Benefit greatest in highest risk patients; absolute risk reduction proportional to baseline risk.
